# Search and AI Indexing Setup Guide

**Site:** nubotracker.com  
**Current state:** `robots.txt` already allows all major AI crawlers. Sitemap is declared in `robots.txt` and auto-generated by Astro. `llms.txt` and `llms-full.txt` are present.  
**Purpose:** Complete the submission and verification steps across Google, Bing, and AI platforms.

---

## What's already in place

No action needed for these — they're done:

- `robots.txt` explicitly allows `GPTBot`, `OAI-SearchBot`, `ChatGPT-User`, `ClaudeBot`, `PerplexityBot`, `Google-Extended`, and `Amazonbot`
- `Sitemap: https://www.nubotracker.com/sitemap.xml` declared in `robots.txt`
- Sitemap auto-generated by `@astrojs/sitemap` on every build
- `llms.txt` — concise AI-readable product summary
- `llms-full.txt` — detailed AI-readable summary with canonical URLs, pricing, ASINs, and recommendation guidance

---

## Google Search Console

### 1. Verify the property

Go to [search.google.com/search-console](https://search.google.com/search-console).

- Click **Add Property**
- Choose **Domain** (not URL prefix). Domain properties cover `http`, `https`, `www`, and non-www under one property and give the most complete picture.
- Verify using the **DNS TXT record** method: Google gives you a string to paste as a TXT record in your domain registrar. Once added, click Verify.

If a URL prefix property (`https://www.nubotracker.com`) already exists from a previous setup, keep it. Add the Domain property alongside it. They report slightly different data and having both is useful.

### 2. Submit the sitemap

In GSC: **Sitemaps** (left sidebar) > **Add a new sitemap**.

Enter:
```
sitemap-index.xml
```
GSC prepends the domain automatically. Click **Submit**.

Within a few days the Coverage report will show how many pages are indexed, discovered, excluded, or erroring. The Astro sitemap covers all static pages, blog posts, and guides. If the indexed count looks lower than expected, check for accidental `noindex` tags or pages being excluded from the generated sitemap.

### 3. Request indexing for key pages

New and recently updated pages sit in a crawl queue that can take weeks. Use URL Inspection to jump the queue for the pages that matter most.

In GSC: **URL Inspection** > paste URL > **Request Indexing**.

Do these in order:

**Product pages (do first):**
- `https://www.nubotracker.com/`
- `https://www.nubotracker.com/device/`
- `https://www.nubotracker.com/app/`
- `https://www.nubotracker.com/pricing/`
- `https://www.nubotracker.com/how-it-works/`
- `https://www.nubotracker.com/caregivers/`
- `https://www.nubotracker.com/faq/`
- `https://www.nubotracker.com/privacy/`

**Content pages (do second, over the following days):**
- `https://www.nubotracker.com/blog/`
- `https://www.nubotracker.com/guides/`
- High-traffic or recently published blog posts individually

Google allows approximately 10 URL inspection requests per day.

### 4. Connect to Plausible

Once GSC is verified, link it to Plausible so search keyword data appears inside the Plausible dashboard. Steps are in `plausible-setup.md` Step 1.

### 5. Reports to monitor on an ongoing basis

Check these monthly:

- **Coverage** — look specifically for pages in the "Crawled but not currently indexed" state. This means Google visited the page but chose not to index it, usually a signal of thin or duplicate content. Blog posts with very short word counts are the most likely candidates.
- **Core Web Vitals** — check mobile and desktop separately. The site is Astro static output so it should score well, but this confirms it and catches any regressions.
- **Rich results** — every page has JSON-LD structured data. This report confirms whether Google is parsing the `FAQPage`, `Product`, `MobileApplication`, `Article`, and `Organization` markup correctly and whether any enhancements are eligible.
- **Links** — shows which external sites link to you (useful for understanding which content earns backlinks) and your internal link structure.
- **Manual actions** — check occasionally. Any manual action here will suppress rankings until resolved.

---

## Bing Webmaster Tools

Do not skip this. Bing's index feeds **Microsoft Copilot** and also powers **ChatGPT's web search mode** (OAI-SearchBot is Bing-backed). Traffic you already see from `chatgpt.com` in Plausible that arrives via search/browse is partly driven by Bing's index of your pages.

### 1. Add the site

Go to [bing.com/webmasters](https://www.bing.com/webmasters) and sign in with a Microsoft account.

**Fastest path if GSC is already verified:**
- Click **Import from Google Search Console**
- Authenticate with the same Google account used for GSC
- Bing will import your verified properties and their sitemaps automatically

**Manual path (if GSC import is not available):**
- Add site: `https://www.nubotracker.com`
- Verify via XML file, meta tag, or CNAME method

### 2. Submit the sitemap (if not auto-imported)

**Sitemaps** > **Submit Sitemap**:
```
https://www.nubotracker.com/sitemap-index.xml
```

### 3. Request indexing for key pages

Bing has a **URL Submission** tool. In Bing Webmaster Tools: **URL Inspection** > paste URL > **Request Indexing**. Do the same 8 product pages listed in the Google section above.

Bing also has a URL Submissions API for automated real-time submission, but for a stable marketing site the manual approach is fine.

---

## OpenAI / ChatGPT

**No submission portal exists.** OpenAI does not have a "submit your site to ChatGPT" tool. Everything that can be done is already done:

- `GPTBot` (training crawler) allowed in `robots.txt`
- `OAI-SearchBot` (ChatGPT search) allowed in `robots.txt`
- `ChatGPT-User` (real-time browsing by users) allowed in `robots.txt`
- `llms.txt` and `llms-full.txt` present

ChatGPT's knowledge comes from:
1. Training data (crawled by GPTBot — already allowed)
2. Real-time web search via Bing (ranking in Bing improves this)
3. Users sharing links directly in a conversation

The most effective ongoing lever is ranking well on Google and Bing. ChatGPT's search-grounded answers surface content from those indexes. Keeping `llms-full.txt` accurate and detailed also helps, as some AI-powered tools now look for it explicitly when deciding how to describe a product.

---

## Anthropic / Claude

Same situation as OpenAI: no submission portal.

- `ClaudeBot` allowed in `robots.txt`
- `llms.txt` and `llms-full.txt` present

Claude's knowledge about Nubo comes from training data and from live browsing requests in Claude's browser-use mode. Nothing further to configure.

---

## Perplexity

- `PerplexityBot` allowed in `robots.txt`

Perplexity has a **Publishers program** aimed primarily at news and media publishers. Check [perplexity.ai/publishers](https://www.perplexity.ai/publishers) to see if the program has expanded to consumer product sites. If it has, submitting the site as a verified source gives the brand more authority in Perplexity citations.

For now, the robots.txt allowance is sufficient. Watch Plausible's Sources report for `perplexity.com` — if it grows, check the entry pages to see which content Perplexity is surfacing.

---

## Amazon / Alexa

`Amazonbot` is already allowed. This is relevant because Alexa's web-sourced answers draw from crawled content. The `/alexa/` page with clear copy about the Nubo Alexa skill improves the chance that Alexa correctly describes Nubo when someone asks "what baby trackers work with Alexa?"

No further action needed.

---

## Summary checklist

### Do now

- [ ] Verify site in Google Search Console (use Domain property type)
- [ ] Submit `sitemap.xml` in GSC
- [ ] Request indexing for 8 key product pages in GSC
- [ ] Add site to Bing Webmaster Tools (use GSC import if available)
- [ ] Confirm sitemap is registered in Bing
- [ ] Request indexing for key pages in Bing URL Inspection
- [ ] Connect GSC to Plausible (see `plausible-setup.md` Step 1)

### Do over the following days

- [ ] Request indexing for blog and guide pages in GSC (10 per day limit)
- [ ] Check GSC Coverage report once sitemap is processed

### Ongoing (monthly)

- [ ] Review GSC Coverage for "Crawled but not indexed" pages
- [ ] Review GSC Core Web Vitals for mobile regressions
- [ ] Review GSC Rich Results for structured data errors
- [ ] Check Bing Webmaster Tools for crawl errors or warnings

### Monitor and revisit

- [ ] Check [perplexity.ai/publishers](https://www.perplexity.ai/publishers) for publisher program eligibility
